intro: Lomelosia rhodopensis, also known as Lilium rhodopae, is a species of flowering plant in the lily family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its use in the treatment of various health issues such as wounds, fever, and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents of Lomelosia rhodopensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its pharmacological effects. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions and respiratory infections.
